go_no_go_recommendation: Proceed with Caution. The project has significant potential but also involves substantial risks related to laser safety, contamination, and achieving performance targets under stress. The 'Builder' scenario is appropriate, but only if the initial safety and baseline measurements are successfully addressed. The project should proceed only if the laser safety interlock system is fully functional and certified, the cleanroom protocols are in place and personnel are trained, and the initial Strehl ratio measurement meets expectations. Failure to meet these initial criteria should trigger a reassessment of the project's feasibility and potential for success.
combined_summary: The three most critical, immediate actions are: 1) Implement a Class 4 laser safety interlock system that automatically shuts down the high-power lasers if any door to the laser testing area is opened by 2026-01-21 17:00, to prevent accidental laser exposure. 2) Establish a cleanroom protocol for all personnel entering the testing area, including the use of appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) such as gowns, gloves, and masks, with a documented training program completed by all personnel by 2026-01-21 17:00, to minimize the risk of contamination of optical surfaces. 3) Establish a baseline Strehl ratio measurement for the 1+6 tile configuration at ambient temperature (22°C +/- 1°C) and atmospheric pressure (1013 hPa +/- 10 hPa) by 2026-01-17 17:00, to provide a reference point for subsequent thermal and vibration stress tests. These actions are essential because they address immediate safety concerns, mitigate a major technical risk (contamination), and establish a critical baseline measurement for evaluating the project's success.

feedback.title: Quantify Thermal-Structural-Optical Scaling Model
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Establish a baseline Strehl ratio measurement for the 1+6 tile configuration at ambient temperature (22°C +/- 1°C) and atmospheric pressure (1013 hPa +/- 10 hPa) by 2026-01-17 17:00. This provides a reference point for subsequent thermal and vibration stress tests.
*   Define the specific mathematical function that will be used to extrapolate the Thermal-Structural-Optical (TSO) scaling model to 19+ tile apertures by 2026-01-21 17:00. This function must include terms for thermal gradients, structural deformation, and optical aberrations, and must be justified based on first-principles physics. This is needed to ensure that the scaling model is based on sound physical principles.
*   Develop a detailed error budget for the TSO scaling model, including contributions from measurement uncertainty, model assumptions, and extrapolation errors by 2026-01-24 17:00. This error budget must be used to quantify the uncertainty bounds on the predicted Strehl ratio and wall-plug efficiency for 19+ tile apertures. This is needed to assess the reliability of the scaling model.
*   Create a simulation of the 19+ tile configuration using the defined mathematical function and error budget by 2026-01-28 17:00. This simulation must predict the Strehl ratio and wall-plug efficiency under worst-case thermal and dynamic loading conditions, and must be used to identify potential design flaws or performance limitations. This is needed to validate the scaling model and identify areas for improvement.

feedback.title: Characterize Heat-Rejection Interface Impedance
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Procure at least three different cold plates with known thermal impedances ranging from 0.1 K/W to 1.0 K/W, measured at the heat-rejection interface by 2026-01-21 17:00. This is needed to simulate a flight-representative range of thermal environments.
*   Design and fabricate a custom mounting interface between the cold plates and the optical engine, ensuring a thermal contact resistance of no more than 0.05 K/W by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to minimize thermal losses and ensure accurate temperature control.
*   Develop a calibration procedure for measuring the thermal impedance of the heat-rejection interface in-situ, using at least three calibrated thermocouples with an accuracy of +/- 0.1°C by 2026-01-28 17:00. This is needed to accurately characterize the thermal environment of the optical engine.
*   Perform a series of thermal tests with each cold plate, measuring the temperature distribution across the optical engine and the heat flux through the heat-rejection interface by 2026-01-31 17:00. This is needed to validate the thermal model of the optical engine and the accuracy of the heat-rejection interface characterization.

feedback.title: Define Vibration Spectra and Tolerances
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Obtain flight-representative vibration spectra from at least three different spacecraft missions with similar payload characteristics by 2026-01-17 17:00. This is needed to define the vibration environment for the stress test.
*   Define the specific power spectral density (PSD) profiles for the injected vibration, including reaction-wheel bands (10-100 Hz), broadband microvibration (100 Hz - 5 kHz), and slewing transients by 2026-01-21 17:00. The PSD profiles must be documented and justified based on the flight-representative vibration spectra.
*   Establish acceptable tolerance levels for alignment and phasing shifts under vibration, specifying a maximum allowable shift of 10% of the wavelength (e.g., 100 nm for a 1 micron wavelength) by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to quantify the performance degradation due to vibration.
*   Procure at least three calibrated accelerometers with a sensitivity of at least 10 mV/g and a frequency response of at least 10 kHz by 2026-01-28 17:00. These accelerometers must be used to monitor the vibration environment at the bench interface and to verify that the injected vibration spectra meet the defined PSD profiles. This is needed to ensure that the stress test is representative of the flight environment.

feedback.title: Establish Stray-Light Measurement Protocol
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Procure a calibrated integrating sphere with a diameter of at least 10 cm and a reflectivity of at least 99% at the operating wavelength by 2026-01-17 17:00. This is needed to accurately measure the total stray light level in the vacuum chamber.
*   Develop a detailed stray-light measurement protocol, including the specific locations for placing the integrating sphere and the procedure for measuring the stray-light level as a function of input power by 2026-01-21 17:00. This protocol must be documented and justified based on the geometry of the vacuum chamber and the optical layout of the beam combining system.
*   Define the acceptable stray-light level at the detector, specifying a maximum allowable stray-light power of 10% of the signal power by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to ensure that the SNR is sufficient for accurate seam phasing.
*   Perform a backscatter/SNR burn-down test with the final beam dump, shrouding, and baffle configuration installed, measuring the stray-light level at the detector as a function of input power by 2026-01-28 17:00. This test must be used to verify that the stray-light level meets the defined acceptance criteria before full array integration.

feedback.title: Implement Laser Safety Interlock System
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Design and implement a Class 4 laser safety interlock system that automatically shuts down the high-power lasers if any door to the laser testing area is opened by 2026-01-21 17:00. This is needed to prevent accidental laser exposure to personnel.
*   Install redundant laser power monitors that continuously measure the output power of each laser and trigger an automatic shutdown if the power exceeds a pre-set threshold by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to prevent overexposure to laser radiation.
*   Conduct a comprehensive laser safety training program for all personnel involved in the project, covering laser hazards, safety procedures, and emergency response protocols by 2026-01-28 17:00. This is needed to ensure that all personnel are aware of the risks and how to mitigate them.
*   Establish a written laser safety standard operating procedure (SOP) that includes details of the laser system, potential hazards, control measures, and emergency procedures by 2026-01-31 17:00. This is needed to ensure that all personnel follow consistent safety practices.

feedback.title: Qualify Vacuum Chamber Safety Systems
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Certify the vacuum chamber's pressure relief system to ensure it can safely vent the chamber in case of over-pressurization, with a relief valve set to no more than 110% of the chamber's maximum operating pressure by 2026-01-21 17:00. This is needed to prevent catastrophic failure of the chamber.
*   Implement a redundant vacuum monitoring system with audible and visual alarms that activate if the chamber pressure deviates from the specified operating range by more than 10% by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to detect and respond to vacuum leaks or other pressure anomalies.
*   Develop and implement a written procedure for safely venting the vacuum chamber, including steps to prevent rapid influx of air that could damage sensitive equipment by 2026-01-28 17:00. This is needed to ensure that the chamber is vented in a controlled manner.
*   Ensure that the vacuum chamber is equipped with an emergency shutdown system that can be activated from multiple locations, immediately cutting power to all electrical equipment inside the chamber by 2026-01-31 17:00. This is needed to quickly de-energize the system in case of an emergency.

feedback.title: Establish Contamination Control Protocol
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Establish a cleanroom protocol for all personnel entering the testing area, including the use of appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE) such as gowns, gloves, and masks, with a documented training program completed by all personnel by 2026-01-21 17:00. This is needed to minimize the risk of contamination of optical surfaces.
*   Implement a bakeout procedure for all components that will be placed inside the vacuum chamber, including a minimum bakeout temperature of 150°C for at least 24 hours, with documentation of the temperature and duration for each component by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to remove volatile contaminants from the components.
*   Install a residual gas analyzer (RGA) in the vacuum chamber to monitor the partial pressures of various contaminants, with alarm thresholds set for specific contaminants such as water vapor, hydrocarbons, and oxygen by 2026-01-28 17:00. This is needed to detect and respond to contamination events.
*   Establish a procedure for cleaning optical surfaces inside the vacuum chamber, using only approved cleaning solvents and techniques, with documentation of the cleaning procedure and the results of pre- and post-cleaning inspection by 2026-01-31 17:00. This is needed to maintain the performance of the optical system.

feedback.title: Define Hazardous Material Handling Procedures
feedback.description:
To initiate this project, you must:
*   Identify all hazardous materials that will be used in the project, including cleaning solvents, cryogenic fluids (e.g., liquid nitrogen), and laser dyes, and obtain Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for each material by 2026-01-17 17:00. This is needed to understand the hazards associated with each material.
*   Develop written procedures for the safe handling, storage, and disposal of each hazardous material, including the use of appropriate PPE, ventilation, and spill containment measures by 2026-01-21 17:00. This is needed to prevent accidental exposure or release of hazardous materials.
*   Provide training to all personnel who will be handling hazardous materials, covering the hazards, safe handling procedures, and emergency response protocols by 2026-01-24 17:00. This is needed to ensure that all personnel are aware of the risks and how to mitigate them.
*   Establish a spill response plan that includes procedures for containing and cleaning up spills of hazardous materials, as well as procedures for reporting spills to the appropriate authorities by 2026-01-28 17:00. This is needed to minimize the impact of spills on personnel and the environment.
